debug_points.description: The images show a street-level view that appears to be heavily obscured by lens blur or obstruction (possibly vehicle hood or equipment). Visible portions show cobblestone/paver sidewalk with white-painted curb, asphalt roadway, and a white bus/truck. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible in any of the frames.

All images show a large puddle of standing water covering most of the road surface. While the water is extensive, there is no visible evidence of actual potholes - no exposed base material, no broken edges, no visible depth or holes breaking the water surface. The yellow curb visible in image 3 shows normal infrastructure.

These images appear to be taken from an extremely low or unusual angle, possibly from near ground level or a malfunctioning camera. Most frames show primarily a gray/beige uniform surface (possibly a vehicle hood or obscured lens) with only partial views of sidewalks and curbs at the edges. No road surface is clearly visible to assess for potholes.

The images show a flooded street area with significant standing water covering most of the road surface. While there is extensive water pooling visible throughout all angles, I cannot confirm any actual potholes because the water obscures the road surface completely - no holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible breaking through the water surface.

These images show a parking area with wet pavement and a white vehicle (appears to be a van or small truck) parked to the south/southwest. The road surface shows moisture/water pooling but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The grey areas visible in most images appear to be wet asphalt or standing water on intact pavement.

This appears to be Street View imagery captured from above water - likely from a boat or watercraft on the Panama Canal or adjacent waterway. The murky greenish-brown water dominates most frames, with some vegetation (palm-like plants) and a brick structure visible at the water's edge in the northeast-facing images. No road surface is visible in any of the 9 images.

These images appear to be taken from an unusual angle, possibly looking down at pavement/sidewalk with most of the frame showing a grey featureless surface (possibly a blurred or obstructed camera view). The visible portions show concrete sidewalk with diagonal tile pattern, a curved concrete planter or barrier, and asphalt road surface. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible.

This location appears to be capturing a flooded or waterlogged paved area, likely a plaza, parking area, or wide intersection. Standing water covers most of the visible surface area in all directions. The visible pavement edges (concrete/asphalt) around the water perimeter show weathering and surface deterioration but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible.

The images show a flooded street with extensive standing water covering most of the road surface. While the water pooling is significant, I cannot see any actual holes, exposed base material, or broken edges beneath the water - only a wet road surface. The yellow-painted curb and sidewalk are visible in several frames.

This appears to be a parking lot with marked parking spaces. The images show concrete/asphalt surface with yellow parking line markings. A vehicle (beige/champagne colored sedan) is visible in the southeast view. The surface appears weathered but intact without visible potholes or holes with missing asphalt.

The images show a wet road surface with significant water pooling/puddles visible across multiple angles. While there is standing water present, I cannot confirm any actual potholes - no visible holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, jagged edges, or measurable depth breaking through the water surface. The road appears to be standard asphalt with water accumulation.

Images show a large puddle of standing water on an asphalt surface near a curb with yellow paint. While the water pooling is significant, there is no visible hole, depression with exposed base material, or broken edges visible. The water surface appears smooth and uniform, obscuring what is underneath but not revealing any actual pothole structure.

The images show a paved road surface with significant standing water or flooding covering most of the visible road area. The road surface visible above the water line appears to be standard asphalt in reasonable condition. A vehicle tire is visible in image 6, indicating this is a trafficked road area.

The images show a parking area or street with several parked vehicles. The road surface appears to be concrete or light-colored asphalt in fair condition. Most views are heavily obscured by camera distortion or blur, but visible portions show no potholes or holes with missing asphalt.

These images show a boat or watercraft perspective on murky water, not a road surface. The camera appears to be positioned on water looking at a shoreline/embankment with grass and a concrete or stone edge. No road infrastructure is visible in any of the 9 images.

The images show a parking area with vehicles and asphalt/concrete surfaces. Most images are heavily obscured by a large grey blur or distortion (likely a stitching/rendering artifact from Street View). The visible portions show intact asphalt with some surface wear, oil stains, and minor discoloration, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible.

This location shows a private swimming pool area with wooden deck, not a public road. Images 2-9 show a blue-tiled swimming pool with wooden decking, lounge chairs, personal items (camera, sandals, shoes), and pool features. Image 1 shows what appears to be a grey surface (possibly pool coping or concrete edge) with water. No road infrastructure or potholes are present.

These images appear to be captured from an extremely low angle or possibly underwater/submerged position. The grey-beige uniform area dominating most frames is murky water or an obscured lens, not road surface. Where concrete/asphalt is visible (upper portions of images 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8), the road surface appears intact with normal wear patterns and expansion joints.

These images appear to be taken from a very low angle, possibly a camera mounted very close to the ground or malfunctioning. The views show paved road surface with white road markings (directional arrows visible in images 5 and 6) and curb edges. No potholes or holes with missing asphalt are visible - the road surface appears intact with normal wear.

The images show a paved road surface with what appears to be wet conditions or a dark surface. The views are predominantly showing the road surface at close range, with most images showing either grey pavement or dark areas that appear to be shadows/wet surfaces rather than actual potholes. No visible holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are observable.
